summary: chlklib typosquats chalk and executes remote code on import
details: Package name `chlklib` is a one-edit typosquat of the popular `chalk` library and re-exports a chalk-shaped API (`Chalk`, `chalkStderr`, `colorNames`). The main entry `dist/index.cjs` invokes a default export from `dist/vendor/original-color/index.cjs` at top level, so the payload fires whenever any consumer `require()`s or `import`s the package. That payload performs an HTTP request to `https://funnystore.org/lib/index.php`, XOR-decrypts the response body with the hardcoded key `9laGwEpZ6G/hLnZVkI+dIumkvz4/E8QDsGvpNuBRZ3O9cZLJOtWMDTp9J39FAO9p`, and `eval`s the plaintext â€” granting the operator of funnystore.org arbitrary code execution on every installer that loads the package. The XOR layer over an HTTP fetch is anti-analysis: it hides the served JavaScript from network inspection and from static scanners. The behavior is unrelated to the package's purported purpose (terminal coloring) and matches a typosquat-dropper supply-chain attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
